title: Caladenia ornata
taxonomy: Caladenia ornata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a species of orchid native to Australia. uses: Caladenia ornata has been traditionally used in Australian Indigenous medicine to treat various ailments. intro: Caladenia ornata, commonly known as the ornate spider orchid, is a species of orchid native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Caladenia ornata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
